title: betula alba subsp. verrucosa
intro: Betula alba subsp. verrucosa, also known as the warty birch, is a subspecies of the white birch tree, characterized by the presence of warty growths on its bark. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which make it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Betula alba subsp. verrucosa include reducing inflammation, improving skin health, and supporting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Betula alba subsp. verrucosa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
